content.problemStatement: Read problems statements in Russian also.Chef plays with the sequence of **N** numbers. During a single move Chef is able to choose a non-decreasing subsequence of the sequence and to remove it from the sequence. Help him to remove all the numbers in the minimal number of moves.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines. The first line contains an integer N, the length of the sequence. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimal number of moves required to delete the whole sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The whole sequence is already non-decreasing, so it can be removed in a single move.
One optimal way: first remove subsequence 1 2 3, then remove the remaining element 4.
The sequence is strictly decreasing, so each element must be removed in a separate move.

solution.approach: The problem can be transformed into finding the length of the longest increasing subsequence (LIS) in the given sequence. To do this, we first transform the sequence by negating each element. Then, we use dynamic programming to find the length of the LIS in the transformed sequence, which corresponds to the minimal number of moves required to delete the whole sequence in the original problem.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the array used to store the tails of the LIS.
